class Solution {
public:
    string breakPalindrome(string palindrome) {
        int n = palindrome.length();
        if (n == 1) return "";
        for (int i = 0; i < n/2; ++i) {
            if (palindrome[i] != 'a') {
                palindrome[i] = 'a';
                return palindrome;
            }
        }
        // All the first half are 'a's. Change last character to 'b'.
        palindrome.back() = 'b';
        return palindrome;
    }
};

class Solution {
public:
    string breakPalindrome(string palindrome) {
        int n = palindrome.length();
        if (n == 1) return "";
        for (int i = 0; i < n/2; ++i) {
            if (palindrome[i] != 'a') {
                palindrome[i] = 'a';
                return palindrome;
            }
        }
        palindrome.back() = 'b';
        return palindrome;
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    string breakPalindrome(string palindrome) {
        int n = palindrome.length();
        if (n == 1) return "";
        for (int i = 0; i < n/2; ++i) {
            if (palindrome[i] != 'a') {
                palindrome[i] = 'a';
                return palindrome;
            }
        }
        palindrome.back() = 'b';
        return palindrome;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The algorithm efficiently breaks the palindrome by iterating through the first half of the string. If a non-'a' character is found, it is replaced with 'a' to achieve the lexicographically smallest result. If all first-half characters are 'a', the last character is changed to 'b'. This ensures minimal lexicographical order and optimal performance.
